ReleaseLabel: Extension pre-0.35.0 (Set 079 'Copilot Seat-Profile Onboarding and Verification-Mode Copy', Session 5 of 5)
Notes: Ad-hoc per-set UAT for Set 079's two features: (1) the guided Full-tier 'Provider access' sub-choice in the Getting Started form that sets up Set 078's copilot-cli seat profile end-to-end (probe, scaffold-then-refresh sequencing, cancellable progress, honest failure messages, transport.profile write), and (2) the simplified Lightweight verification-mode radio copy. Authored to the Set 078 bar: every HumanAction is a literal command or exact UI element label; every Expectation is a literal string or value to diff against; every quoted string was re-grounded against CURRENT source during authoring (gettingStartedHtml.js constants, copilotSeatSetup.ts describeSeatSetupOutcome, gitScaffold.ts rerunRefreshHint/progress titles), and every cited Layer-2 suite was re-run fresh during authoring with the exact pass count quoted per walk. UAT runs against a LOCALLY BUILT VSIX ONLY (from tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration run `npx vsce package`, then 'Extensions: Install from VSIX...') -- never a published build (the Set 058 lesson). Walks 1-3 and 6 are webview walks that cannot be driven headless from this environment; their derived logic (render, default-checked state, persistence precedence, warning visibility) is fully pinned at Layer 2 and the genuinely operator-only portion (real webview teardown/reload, real notifications) is named per walk. Walks 4 and 5 are REAL seat runs: the orchestrator already ran the same code paths for real during Sessions 2-3 on this same seat (S2: guided happy path, SUCCESS in 102 s, 15/18 models, providers anthropic/google/openai; S3: induced missing-binary run and a REAL mid-run cancel with process-tree kill and no partial lockfile -- see s2-close-reason.md / s3-close-reason.md), so you are re-running known-good scenarios through the actual form UI, not exploring unknown territory. Walk 4 makes about 18 short real calls against your Copilot seat (roughly 1-2 minutes); Walk 5 deliberately cancels partway through a second run. HONEST SCOPE LIMITS, carried from the spec's M3 note: (a) this checklist validates a SINGLE personal Copilot seat -- the same seat Set 078 used; it does NOT validate multi-seat or enterprise-seat model availability, and an enterprise-locked seat exposing only one provider family would fail the >=2-provider check even though the guided flow 'worked'. (b) This walk machine has DABBLER_* provider keys set, so every failure message you see will be the keys-present variant ('...keep the api profile working'); the keyless variants ('the router is not yet functional') cannot be honestly walked on a keyed machine and are pinned instead by the Layer-2 honesty matrix in copilotSeatSetup.test.ts (5 outcome kinds x keyed/keyless, positive AND negative assertions). (c) The POSIX cancel branch (detached process-group kill) is unit-pinned but not walkable on this Windows host. (d) No genuine auth-failure walk, same explicit operator decision as Set 078 (deauthenticating a real working seat was ruled out as unsafe). Scaffold walks (4 and 5) each use a FRESH EMPTY FOLDER, one per walk.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 -- the Full-tier 'Provider access' sub-choice renders
Review.ItemLabel: Picking Full reveals the provider-access radio group with the API option pre-checked; picking Lightweight removes it entirely
Review.HumanAction: Open a FRESH EMPTY FOLDER in VS Code (locally built VSIX installed). Click the Dabbler AI Orchestration icon in the Activity Bar and look at the Getting Started form's step 1. The radio labeled exactly "Full" is checked by default. Look directly under the tier radios. Then click the radio labeled exactly "Lightweight" and look again. Then click "Full" once more.
Review.Expectation:
With Full checked, a radio group appears labeled exactly: "Provider access (how routed calls run)" with exactly two options, in this order:
1. "Direct provider API keys — calls use your DABBLER_* provider API keys (the default)." -- this one is PRE-CHECKED.
2. "GitHub Copilot CLI seat — calls run through your Copilot subscription's command-line tool; no provider API keys needed." -- unchecked.
The group sits BETWEEN the tier radios and the "Verification budget (USD, not-to-exceed)" input. With Lightweight checked, the whole "Provider access" group disappears (in its place Lightweight shows its own "Verification (per session set)" group -- covered in Walk 6). Clicking Full again brings the group back with "Direct provider API keys" still checked.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "Set 079 S1"` fresh during authoring: 20/20 passing, including 'renders on Full with api checked by default and both copy texts', 'returns empty string for the Lightweight tier (block omitted, not hidden)', and 'sits in step 1 between the tier radios and the budget block' (gettingStartedHtml.test.ts suite 'transport-profile block (Set 079 S1)'). All three on-screen strings above are quoted verbatim from gettingStartedHtml.js:114-120 (TRANSPORT_PROFILE_LABEL_TEXT / _API_TEXT / _COPILOT_TEXT), re-read moments before this checklist was written. The real webview rendering in an actual VS Code window is the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 2 -- the Copilot pick persists across re-expand and window reload
Review.ItemLabel: An explicitly selected 'GitHub Copilot CLI seat' radio survives hiding the view and a full window reload
Review.HumanAction: In the same folder as Walk 1, with Full checked, click the radio labeled "GitHub Copilot CLI seat — calls run through your Copilot subscription's command-line tool; no provider API keys needed." Collapse/hide the Session Set Explorer view and re-expand it. Then run 'Developer: Reload Window' from the command palette. Re-open the Getting Started form.
Review.Expectation: After BOTH the re-expand and the full window reload, the "GitHub Copilot CLI seat" radio is still checked -- it does not snap back to "Direct provider API keys". The Full tier radio is also still checked. (Side note, pinned but not observable on this machine: while the Copilot option is selected, the Full-tier missing-provider-key warning is suppressed even when no DABBLER_* key exists -- the key warning belongs to the direct-API option only. Your machine has keys set, so that warning never shows for you either way.)
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Same fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "Set 079 S1"` run: 20/20 passing, including the 8-test 'restoreGsState seat-profile seed (Set 079 S1)' sub-suite ('a post-seed explicit flip survives the SAME seed (profileDirty)', 'a CHANGED seed re-applies over a dirty flip and clears the flag', 'cross-root discard resets the profile fields too') -- the same seed/dirty/reload precedence contract Set 077 established for the tier and verification-mode radios. The key-warning suppression is pinned by 'env warning HIDDEN while Copilot is selected, even with no keys' in the 'D6 key warning vs the Copilot seat pick (Set 079 S1)' suite. The real vscode setState/getState round-trip across an actual window reload is inherently untestable on the vscode-stub and is the operator portion -- it is exactly what this walk exists to confirm.

Review.Subarea: Walk 3 -- missing-Copilot-CLI warning (induced via the setting)
Review.ItemLabel: Pointing copilotCliPath at a nonexistent executable makes the warning appear -- only while the Copilot option is selected
Review.HumanAction: In the same folder, open Settings (workspace scope) and set `dabblerSessionSets.copilotCliPath` to exactly: C:\nonexistent\copilot.exe -- then run 'Developer: Reload Window' (the probe reads the environment at launch). Open the form: Full tier, then click the "GitHub Copilot CLI seat" radio if it is not already selected (it should still be, from Walk 2). Read the text inside the provider-access block. Then click "Direct provider API keys" and look again. Finally, CLEAN UP: delete the `dabblerSessionSets.copilotCliPath` setting and reload the window once more -- the warning must then stay gone even with the Copilot option selected (your real `copilot` CLI is on PATH).
Review.Expectation: With the bogus setting and the Copilot option selected, a warning banner renders this exact text: "The GitHub Copilot CLI was not found on this machine. The Copilot seat option runs AI calls through the copilot command, which must be installed and on PATH. Install the GitHub Copilot CLI (see the Copilot CLI guide at docs.github.com/copilot), or set the dabblerSessionSets.copilotCliPath setting to the installed copilot executable. Then reload the VS Code window (changes made after launch are not visible until you reload)." The warning is visible ONLY while the Copilot radio is selected -- switching to "Direct provider API keys" hides it. An explicitly configured path DECIDES ALONE: the warning shows even though the real copilot CLI is on your PATH, because the setting points at a file that does not exist. After the cleanup reload, no warning shows with Copilot selected.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Same fresh 20/20 'Set 079 S1' run, including the 6-test 'missing-Copilot-CLI warning (Set 079 S1)' suite ('warning VISIBLE when Copilot is selected AND the CLI is missing', 'warning hidden while the API option is selected, even with the CLI missing', 'copilotWarningHtml renders role=alert'). The warning string is quoted verbatim from gettingStartedHtml.js:128-135 (COPILOT_WARNING_TEXT). The explicit-setting-decides-alone probe semantics are documented and implemented at copilotCli.ts:100-121 (an absolute configured path is checked with fileExists and never falls back to PATH). Session 3's real induced-failure dogfood Run A drove the refresh with a nonexistent binary against the real CLI stack (0/18 confirmed, providers=[], honest failure message) -- see s3-close-reason.md. The real settings-UI round trip and host relaunch are the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 4 -- REAL guided happy path on your seat
Review.ItemLabel: A Full + Copilot Build scaffolds the project, probes the seat (~18 real calls), and writes transport.profile: copilot-cli only after confirming >=2 provider families
Review.HumanAction: Open a FRESH EMPTY FOLDER (no bogus copilotCliPath setting -- Walk 3 cleaned it up). In the Getting Started form: leave "Full" checked; click "GitHub Copilot CLI seat"; type 5 into the input labeled exactly "Verification budget (USD, not-to-exceed)". Click the button labeled exactly "Build project structure". First a notification titled "Building project structure…" runs (not cancellable); when it completes, a SECOND progress notification appears titled exactly: "Setting up the Copilot seat — probing the seat's models (about 1–2 minutes)…" with a Cancel affordance. DO NOT cancel -- let it finish (Session 2's real run took 102 seconds on this seat).
Review.Expectation:
An info notification appears reading exactly:
Copilot seat set up: 15/18 models confirmed (providers: anthropic, google, openai). transport.profile: copilot-cli written to ai_router/router-config.yaml.
(As in the Set 078 walk, the exact counts can drift slightly if GitHub changes the CLI's model list -- what matters is: the message starts with 'Copilot seat set up:', the confirmed count is close to 15-18, and all three provider names anthropic/google/openai appear.) Then verify on disk: (a) open ai_router/router-config.yaml, find the `transport:` block -- its `profile:` line now reads `profile: copilot-cli`; (b) the file ai_router/copilot-catalog.lock exists; (c) the normal scaffold artifacts are present (.venv/, docs/session-sets/, the AI-agent instruction files).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran this exact happy path for real during Session 2 with the real venv python + real GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.68 against a scratch project on THIS seat (the same single personal seat as Set 078): SUCCESS in 102 s, 15/18 models confirmed, providers [anthropic, google, openai], transport.profile written by the anchored render, lockfile round-trips (s2-close-reason.md, 'Real-seat dogfood'). Fresh Layer-2 runs during authoring: `npm run test:unit -- --grep "gitScaffold"` 25/25 passing (includes 'buildProjectStructureNoPrompt (the REAL build path)' pinning scaffold-then-refresh sequencing against the real build function, and 'decideCopilotSeatSetup (the sequencing gate)'); `npm run test:unit -- --grep "copilotSeatSetup"` 82/82 passing (result parsing from providers=[...] stdout and lockfile -- never the exit code -- plus the anchored transport.profile replacement that refuses to append a second key). The success-message string is quoted verbatim from copilotSeatSetup.ts:762-764; the progress title from gitScaffold.ts:745-746. What only you can confirm: the real notification sequence and the real end-to-end run through the actual form UI (S2's dogfood drove performCopilotSeatSetup directly, not via form clicks).

Review.ItemLabel: Cancelling the seat probe mid-run kills the child process tree, restores the lockfile state, keeps transport.profile: api, and says so honestly
Review.HumanAction: Open a SECOND FRESH EMPTY FOLDER. Same picks as Walk 4: Full, "GitHub Copilot CLI seat", budget 5, click "Build project structure". This time, when the notification titled "Setting up the Copilot seat — probing the seat's models (about 1–2 minutes)…" appears, click its Cancel button after roughly 15-30 seconds (mid-probe).
Review.Expectation:
A warning notification appears reading exactly:
Copilot seat setup was cancelled — the lockfile was restored to its pre-run state and router-config.yaml keeps transport.profile: api. Meanwhile the DABBLER_* provider key(s) already set keep the api profile working. Re-run seat setup (no need to re-scaffold): .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m ai_router.copilot_catalog --refresh --seat-id seat-<12 hex chars> --seat-label "<this folder's name>"
(The seat-id is a stable 12-hex-character hash derived from your hostname+username -- the same value every run on this machine; the seat-label is the folder's basename. You see the 'Meanwhile the DABBLER_*...' sentence because this machine has provider keys set -- on a keyless machine this same message instead says the router is not yet functional.) Then verify on disk: (a) ai_router/copilot-catalog.lock does NOT exist (the pre-run state in a fresh folder was 'absent', and cancel restored it -- no partial lockfile); (b) ai_router/router-config.yaml's transport `profile:` still reads `api`; (c) the scaffold itself is intact (.venv/, docs/, instruction files) -- cancel only aborted the seat probe, not the project.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran this exact scenario for real during Session 3 (dogfood Run B, driver against the real CLI): cancel at 15 s with two live descendant processes -- after cancel the python pid and BOTH descendants were confirmed dead (taskkill /T tree-kill verified against the real process tree) and no partial lockfile remained (s3-close-reason.md, 'Real induced-failure dogfood'). Fresh `npm run test:unit -- --grep "copilotSeatSetup"` during authoring: 82/82 passing, including cancel-kills-child-and-restores-lockfile, the dispatchKill strategy table (win32 taskkill /T, POSIX group-kill, plain fallback), and the 5-outcome-kinds x keyed/keyless honesty matrix asserting in BOTH directions that no keyless failure message ever implies api 'still works'. The cancelled-message string is composed by the pure describeSeatSetupOutcome (copilotSeatSetup.ts:805-816) and quoted here in its keys-present variant; the re-run hint format is rerunRefreshHint (gitScaffold.ts:592-607). What only you can confirm: the real Cancel button on a real notification, driven from the actual form.

Review.ItemLabel: The Lightweight verification-mode radios carry the new plain-language descriptions with Manual review still marked and checked as the default
Review.HumanAction: In any of the walk folders, open the Getting Started form and click the radio labeled exactly "Lightweight". Read the radio group that appears, labeled exactly "Verification (per session set)".
Review.Expectation:
The group shows exactly two options with these exact descriptions:
1. "Manual review (default) — paste a review prompt into a second AI assistant yourself and record what it says." -- this one is PRE-CHECKED.
2. "Separate verification sessions — a dedicated session on a different AI engine or provider reviews the work before the set can close." -- unchecked.
Neither description contains the old jargon ('structured verification sessions', 'close-out gate', 'record its verdict by hand'). The "Provider access (how routed calls run)" group from Walk 1 is NOT shown anywhere while Lightweight is selected.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "verification-mode block"` fresh during authoring: 5/5 passing, including 'pins the simplified verification-mode copy (Set 079 S4)' (asserts both exact strings above and that verificationModeBlockHtml renders them) and 'renders both radios on Lightweight with the default checked'. Both strings are quoted verbatim from gettingStartedHtml.js:83-88 (VERIFICATION_MODE_OUT_OF_BAND_TEXT / VERIFICATION_MODE_DEDICATED_TEXT), re-read moments before this checklist was written; Session 4 verified the 'dedicated' description against actual Mode B behavior before locking it (no automation overclaim -- s4-close-reason.md). The real rendered webview text is the operator portion.
